jokes.text: They will be changing their slogan from 'The marines are looking for a few good men' to... 'I think sarge likes me.'
jokes.setup: Military gay ban debate and Marine slogan
jokes.punchline: Innocent recruitment slogan becomes suggestive
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that cleverly subverts familiar military recruiting language. Well-structured with clean setup/payoff.

dialogue.line: They will be changing their slogan from 'The marines are looking for a few good men' to... 'I think sarge likes me.'

jokes.text: Will they take the M.A.S.H episodes and edit out Klinger?
jokes.setup: Military gay ban enforcement
jokes.punchline: Cross-dressing character would be problematic
jokes.explanation: Smart cultural reference connecting policy to pop culture, though feels somewhat expected.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Will they take the M.A.S.H episodes and edit out Klinger?

jokes.text: The schools have switched to the more formal, prettier, carnation-covered metal detectors.
jokes.setup: Prom season in high schools
jokes.punchline: Metal detectors get prom makeover
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that brilliantly contrasts prom romance with school violence reality. Great visual comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: The schools have switched to the more formal, prettier, carnation-covered metal detectors.

jokes.text: Do not flip around. No flipping.
jokes.setup: Standard 'don't change the channel' sign-off
jokes.punchline: Desperate repetition reveals insecurity
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Larry's neurotic need for control. The repetition reveals his desperation.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Do not flip around. No flipping.

jokes.text: I slept with Francine last night.
jokes.setup: Larry asking if he made a mistake on the show
jokes.punchline: The 'mistake' is sleeping with his ex-wife
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that completely recontextualizes the opening. Larry's neurotic guilt takes an unexpected turn.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I slept with Francine last night.

jokes.text: It was the entire weekend.
jokes.setup: Artie assuming it was one drunken night
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals it was much more involved
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes Larry's situation progressively worse. Simple but effective cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: It was the entire weekend.

jokes.text: That woman smashed your People's Choice Award. Why take it out on the award? The fucking people gave you that award.
jokes.setup: Artie outraged about Larry sleeping with Francine
jokes.punchline: He's more upset about the award than the relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie logic - he cares more about show business than human relationships. Great character prioritization comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: That woman smashed your People's Choice Award. Why take it out on the award? The fucking people gave you that award.

jokes.text: Big mistake. Big, big mistake.
jokes.setup: Hank commenting on Larry's situation
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase of doom
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank catchphrase. Works purely through character familiarity and Tambor's delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Big mistake. Big, big mistake.

jokes.text: He's gayer than a French horn.
jokes.setup: Debate about whether Alec Baldwin is gay
jokes.punchline: Bizarre musical instrument comparison
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd comparison. The specificity of 'French horn' makes it memorable and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: He's gayer than a French horn.

jokes.text: I had sex with him. Will that be a problem for you?
jokes.setup: Casual revelation after gay debate
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact delivery of bombshell information
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of devastating information. The casual follow-up question makes it even more awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: I had sex with him. Will that be a problem for you?

jokes.text: Ask if the Saab's clutch is still sticking.
jokes.setup: Larry saying he'll tell Alec she said hi
jokes.punchline: Intimate automotive detail reveals relationship depth
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific detail that shows how well she knew Alec. The mundane automotive concern is perfectly awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: Ask if the Saab's clutch is still sticking.

jokes.text: When you eat at Hank's, you and your food... are going... on an adventure.
jokes.setup: Explaining the rotating restaurant concept
jokes.punchline: Grandiose language for spinning while eating
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank grandiosity applied to ridiculous restaurant concept. His earnestness makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: When you eat at Hank's, you and your food... are going... on an adventure.

jokes.text: So I'd like you to take your opinion and shove it right up your ass. Regards, Hank Kingsley. Fax that back to him.
jokes.setup: Business manager says rotating restaurant won't work
jokes.punchline: Professional sign-off after crude insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect juxtaposition of professional courtesy with personal insult. The formal 'Regards' makes it absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: So I'd like you to take your opinion and shove it right up your ass. Regards, Hank Kingsley. Fax that back to him.

jokes.text: Get Chuck Woolery on the phone.
jokes.setup: Hank claiming to know powerful people in the business
jokes.punchline: Chuck Woolery as his big-name investor
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Hank's grandiose claims. Chuck Woolery as 'powerful industry figure' is brilliantly delusional.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Get Chuck Woolery on the phone.

jokes.text: These Baldwin boys seem to be multiplying like fucking jackrabbits.
jokes.setup: Multiple Baldwin brothers on talk shows
jokes.punchline: Crude animal comparison for celebrity siblings
jokes.explanation: Typical Artie crudeness applied to industry observation. Good character voice and timely 90s reference.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: These Baldwin boys seem to be multiplying like fucking jackrabbits.

jokes.text: What's he play with? With his ass, I suppose.
jokes.setup: Man with no arms playing piano
jokes.punchline: Crude assumption about how he plays
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that feels lazy. Gets a reaction but lacks sophistication of better show moments.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: What's he play with? With his ass, I suppose.

jokes.text: Remember what happened with all those Carradines?
jokes.setup: Discussing booking too many Baldwin brothers
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous family member booking disaster
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-reference that suggests this show has made this mistake before. Good world-building detail.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Remember what happened with all those Carradines?

jokes.text: I saw them together at a rolling disco party a few years ago.
jokes.setup: How Artie knows about Francine and Alec
jokes.punchline: Artie at a roller disco party
jokes.explanation: Great visual of gruff Artie at a roller disco. The specificity makes it funnier and perfectly in-character.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I saw them together at a rolling disco party a few years ago.

jokes.text: You see his face slobbering in her ear. You see legs all entangled. You hear the grunts.
jokes.setup: Artie describing what Larry will imagine during interview
jokes.punchline: Increasingly graphic sexual imagery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of uncomfortable imagery. Artie's graphic description tortures Larry effectively.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You see his face slobbering in her ear. You see legs all entangled. You hear the grunts.

jokes.text: No. Paula looks like a jerk.
jokes.setup: Larry worried about looking bad for canceling guest
jokes.punchline: Artie shifts blame to booker
jokes.explanation: Classic producer move - shifting responsibility. Shows Artie's pragmatic approach to show business politics.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: No. Paula looks like a jerk.

jokes.text: A little chocolate never hurt anybody.
jokes.setup: Context suggests she's eating while others work
jokes.punchline: Defensive rationalization for snacking
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment showing workplace eating guilt. Relatable but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: A little chocolate never hurt anybody.

jokes.text: I thought he used something else.
jokes.setup: Discussion of pianist playing with his ass
jokes.punchline: Innocent confusion about anatomy
jokes.explanation: Mild callback to earlier crude joke. Beverly's innocence makes it slightly funnier but still crude.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: I thought he used something else.

jokes.text: She worked at Knott's Berry Farm for a short time.
jokes.setup: Question about working on Knots Landing
jokes.punchline: Similar-sounding theme park instead of TV show
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay confusion between TV show and theme park. Simple but effective misdirection.

dialogue.line: She worked at Knott's Berry Farm for a short time.

jokes.text: We had some really, really wild times together. Of course, that was back in crazier days.
jokes.setup: Alec commenting on Francine
jokes.punchline: Oversharing intimate details with her ex-husband
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment where guest overshares. The 'crazier days' suggests wilder behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Alec
dialogue.line: We had some really, really wild times together. Of course, that was back in crazier days.

jokes.text: She's a little flaky. I see why you'd want out.
jokes.setup: Alec continuing to discuss Francine
jokes.punchline: Insulting the ex-wife to current husband
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward guest behavior. Alec's cluelessness about appropriate boundaries creates good cringe.

dialogue.speaker: Alec
dialogue.line: She's a little flaky. I see why you'd want out.

jokes.text: The phrase 'Be my guest' comes to mind.
jokes.setup: Alec asking if Larry has problem with him dating Francine
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic blessing of past relationship
jokes.explanation: Great passive-aggressive response. Larry's forced politeness while seething underneath is perfect character work.

dialogue.line: The phrase 'Be my guest' comes to mind.

jokes.text: Hey, call me Lar.
jokes.setup: Alec asking for favor
jokes.punchline: Overly familiar nickname from someone he dislikes
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's forced friendliness. The nickname feels desperate and phony.

dialogue.speaker: Alec
dialogue.line: Hey, call me Lar.

jokes.text: The people around The country are going, 'He's staying.'
jokes.setup: Larry commenting on audience applause for Baldwin staying
jokes.punchline: Imagining national audience investment in guest decisions
jokes.explanation: Mild self-referential humor about TV show dynamics.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: The people around The country are going, 'He's staying.'

jokes.text: Alec dated my ex-wife Francine.
jokes.setup: Larry breaking fourth wall during interview
jokes.punchline: Publicly announcing personal information
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-sabotage moment. Larry can't help himself from revealing awkward personal details on air.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Alec dated my ex-wife Francine.

jokes.text: I was referring to our charity work with multiple sclerosis. I know. I know. That, too.
jokes.setup: Alec trying to redirect from dating revelation
jokes.punchline: Larry's awkward acknowledgment of both topics
jokes.explanation: Good awkward recovery attempt that fails. The 'that, too' shows Larry can't let it go.

dialogue.speaker: Alec
dialogue.line: I was referring to our charity work with multiple sclerosis.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I know. I know. That, too.

jokes.text: From the time he walked out all I saw was him fucking her. And she was on top. That lazy bastard.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his on-air behavior to Artie
jokes.punchline: Graphic sexual imagery with judgmental commentary
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy showing Larry's jealous imagination. The 'lazy bastard' comment is perfectly petty.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: From the time he walked out all I saw was him fucking her. And she was on top. That lazy bastard.

jokes.text: Those guys don't know jack shit.
jokes.setup: Potential investor mentioning business manager
jokes.punchline: Hank dismissing financial expertise
jokes.explanation: Typical Hank dismissal of professional advice. In-character but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Those guys don't know jack shit.

jokes.text: I dated Francine also.
jokes.setup: Discussion of charity work with Alec and Larry
jokes.punchline: Third person reveals connection to Francine
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that turns awkward situation into nightmare. Simple but devastating timing.

dialogue.speaker: Ed Begley Jr.
dialogue.line: I dated Francine also.

jokes.text: I met her at one of my roller-skating parties.
jokes.setup: Ed explaining how he met Francine
jokes.punchline: Environmental actor having roller-skating parties
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that fits Ed Begley Jr.'s eco-friendly persona. Callback to earlier Artie reference.

dialogue.speaker: Ed Begley Jr.
dialogue.line: I met her at one of my roller-skating parties.

jokes.text: The sign on the wall said 'all skate.' It means anybody can do whatever they want.
jokes.setup: Ed explaining roller rink policy
jokes.punchline: Innocent skating term used as sexual permission
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double meaning that transforms innocent roller rink terminology into sexual permission. Perfectly delivered.

dialogue.speaker: Ed Begley Jr.
dialogue.line: The sign on the wall said 'all skate.' It means anybody can do whatever they want.

jokes.text: So you did it on wheels?
jokes.setup: Ed's 'all skate' explanation
jokes.punchline: Larry's horrified clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that makes the absurd situation even more specific and horrifying for Larry.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: So you did it on wheels?

jokes.text: Has anybody else been with my ex-wife?
jokes.setup: Two guests have revealed dating Francine
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate public inquiry
jokes.explanation: Perfect breakdown moment where Larry loses all professional composure. Desperate and pathetic in best way.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Has anybody else been with my ex-wife?

jokes.text: How about the guy who plays the piano with his ass?
jokes.setup: Larry asking if anyone else dated Francine
jokes.punchline: Reference to earlier guest discussion
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Larry's complete meltdown. The absurd guest becomes part of his paranoia.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: How about the guy who plays the piano with his ass?

jokes.text: It was like a theme show. Who's the third? Me. That would be me. I would be the third.
jokes.setup: Francine counting people she slept with on the show
jokes.punchline: Larry realizing he's being counted as a conquest
jokes.explanation: Brutal moment where Larry realizes he's just another notch. His clarification makes it more pathetic.

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: It was like a theme show. Who's the third?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Me. That would be me. I would be the third.

jokes.text: I didn't sleep with Ed Begley or the man who plays the piano with his ass!
jokes.setup: Francine defending herself
jokes.punchline: Having to specify she didn't sleep with absurd guest
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows how the absurd guest has become part of their relationship drama.

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: I didn't sleep with Ed Begley or the man who plays the piano with his ass!

jokes.text: Legitimate acts Of penetration... Oral sex... And I think Heavy petting, don't you?
jokes.setup: Larry defining parameters for their sexual history lists
jokes.punchline: Increasingly clinical sexual categorization
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Larry's obsessive need for precision in uncomfortable territory.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Legitimate acts Of penetration... Oral sex... And I think Heavy petting, don't you?

jokes.text: If you keep narrowing it down like this, I'm not going to have any names on my list.
jokes.setup: Francine defining what counts for the list
jokes.punchline: Larry admitting his limited sexual experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-deprecating moment that reveals Larry's insecurity about his sexual history compared to Francine's.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: If you keep narrowing it down like this, I'm not going to have any names on my list.

jokes.text: You could throw your wrist out.
jokes.setup: Larry writing his sexual history list quickly
jokes.punchline: Concern about injury from excessive writing/masturbation reference
jokes.explanation: Mild sexual innuendo that works on two levels. Not particularly clever but fits the relationship dynamic.

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: You could throw your wrist out.

jokes.text: Making my Christmas card list.
jokes.setup: Artie asking what Larry is writing
jokes.punchline: Larry lying about making sexual history list
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's embarrassment and deflection instincts.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Making my Christmas card list.

jokes.text: That's a big mistake.
jokes.setup: Artie noting Francine has Larry lying to friends
jokes.punchline: Artie's catchphrase applied to relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Good use of Artie's recurring catchphrase in relationship context. Callback works well.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: That's a big mistake.

jokes.text: You mean Rebecca Arthur. Why? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about actress from Perfect Strangers
jokes.punchline: Beverly's suspicious follow-up questions
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe moment where Beverly suspects Larry's motives. Shows how pathetic his list-making has become.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: You mean Rebecca Arthur.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Why?

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Yeah.

jokes.text: 'Adrienne Barbeau'! Why, you no-good, star-screwing bastard! Get the hell out of here!
jokes.setup: Beverly role-playing Francine's reaction to Larry's list
jokes.punchline: Extreme overreaction to celebrity name
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing how bad Larry's celebrity connections will look. Beverly's performance is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: 'Adrienne Barbeau'! Why, you no-good, star-screwing bastard! Get the hell out of here!

jokes.text: There must be 85 to 100 names.
jokes.setup: Artie seeing Francine's list
jokes.punchline: Massive number reveals extent of her sexual history
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows the scope of Larry's problem. The specific numbers make it more absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: There must be 85 to 100 names.

jokes.text: You're making a mistake. A big, big mistake.
jokes.setup: Larry refusing to look at Francine's list
jokes.punchline: Artie's signature warning phrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect deployment of Artie's catchphrase. The repetition reinforces his character and the situation's doom.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You're making a mistake. A big, big mistake.

jokes.text: I can't believe it took six years for you to find out!
jokes.setup: Francine admitting she dated Alec and Ed to hurt Larry
jokes.punchline: Amazement at how long it took him to discover
jokes.explanation: Brutal revelation that her revenge plan worked perfectly. The timing makes Larry look even more oblivious.

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: I can't believe it took six years for you to find out!

jokes.text: I've been really busy.
jokes.setup: Francine's amazement at how long revenge took
jokes.punchline: Larry's pathetic excuse for missing obvious signs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry response - totally missing the point and making a weak excuse. Classic character obliviousness.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I've been really busy.

jokes.text: Does the restaurant have a big crank outside? That's kind of inconvenient, you rush outside every time you want the floor to turn?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Hank's rotating restaurant
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of restaurant mechanics
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy imagining the impractical mechanics of Hank's restaurant concept.

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: Does the restaurant have a big crank outside? That's kind of inconvenient, you rush outside every time you want the floor to turn?

jokes.text: Which, by the way, is not a piece-of-shit Saab, I might say.
jokes.setup: Larry mentioning time in car with Francine
jokes.punchline: Defensive response about car quality
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier Saab reference. Shows Larry's petty defensiveness about random details.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Which, by the way, is not a piece-of-shit Saab, I might say.

jokes.text: Which, by the way, Is not a piece-of-shit saab, I might say.
jokes.setup: Larry and Francine reconciling
jokes.punchline: Larry defending his car choice defensively
jokes.explanation: Mild callback to earlier car reference showing Larry's petty defensiveness.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Which, by the way, Is not a piece-of-shit saab, I might say.

jokes.text: I've never considered it a charity, but I used to do your ex-wife.
jokes.setup: Fourth Baldwin brother mentioning something in common with Larry
jokes.punchline: Another revelation about Francine
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that makes Larry's nightmare complete. The 'charity' line adds insult to injury.

dialogue.speaker: Daniel Baldwin
dialogue.line: I've never considered it a charity, but I used to do your ex-wife.
